author: @ericclark133
text: I worked for a small insurance company that was over 100 years old. It bought a foosball table, and a ping-pong table, for its employees to use during break. But those were the only such perks it bought - ok, there was a gym too, but that was used as a way to reduce health premiums. The foosball table and the ping pong table are way, way cheaper than giving a raise of $1 per hour - ie, the cost of that was effectively saved within 2-3 hours - that means using the excuse of “employee welfare” to avoid paying more, so it’s a rather cynical move that worked. And the employees never went over 15 minutes - in any case they are required by state law. So nothing was wasted and a ton of money was saved.

author: @clubware
text: Been there. They don't need all of those people. If I had a nickel every time a staff member said "we need a person to do [insert 10 hour a week function here]..." I'd have a lot of nickels. They hire things like a "coffee pourer", "a foosball cleaner", and other useless single function jobs - even if "administrative". The inefficiency is palpable. Facebook has 2 completely separate ad managers, obviously built by different teams, and you can't figure out which to use. If you use one, the changes don't show in the other. It's a train wreck. But both teams are justifying their existence. Why 2 completely different systems? Because the people are there and they have to do something. This is just one example. There's no way they need all of those personnel and it's about time.

author: @shrinkshooter
text: "It's an excuse to remove dead weight." No, it isn't; CEOs are making hugely destructive decisions fueled solely by hopium. I know a senior developer working at Block, lead of a subteam of 6, belong to a team of 19. All his engineers got let go no warning, so it's just him now, and of the 19 on this team, 5 remain. These were good, competent engineers, and so is this guy. These idiot CEOs don't understand the technology and have bought into magical thinking of "if we just leverage models, more productivity for cheaper, instantly! wow!" These aren't HR Karens getting chopped. These are massively atrocious business decisions happening in real time, and you WILL see the chickens come home to roost before the year is out. Silicon Valley is trapped in a delusional fever dream, economic implosion will be the cure.

author: @rman88
text:
Worst part about this is. Its most likely going to backfire. If you look at the satistics. almost 95% of all Ai change overs Fail. Hell need a great example of this, look at Microsoft. A company that decided to use AI to write code. Yes trust the computer program to understand what a computer bug is. Now windows is in shambles and their scratching their heads wondering what went wrong. 

Look, most of these companies are Not actual using ai properly. Their not even taking their time to Slowly introduce ai to their products or even workplace. Their just like, Bam your all fired ai will fix it. Its like firing everyone only to give their work to an intern. It just never works out well. And they will lose a lot of money off this. As they think Ai will fix all the problems in 3 seconds without the need to pay pesky people to do it for you. Only to fail to realize, You still need to spend a shit tone of money to make it work. 

Yes, ai is not actual free. You need to pay to design the ai. You need to pay to either own a dater center or lease one. You need to pay to keep staff on to mange the ai. and you need to accept that your revenue is going to go down. As people in general don't like using ai.

author: @HowardMoth
text:
I think there's a serious problem once again with how everyone is looking at these TikTok videos. Primarily, unless you're a face value thinker, you have to realize that these TikTok Zoomers, or "women in STEM", or whatever you want to call them did not create these workplace environments. The hamster doesn't get to pick which wheel its owner buys from the pet store and neither did these young tech workers. All of this excess comforts in the office space was something generation X and millennials pushed for when the market was up and the getting was good. It's only now that companies are looking to keep the "graph go up" that all these things are being called into question. 

Furthermore, it's way too easy to point at Elon Musk's takeover of Twitter and say, "look he gutted the company and it still works". The real critical question would be what value has he added to the company that wasn't there before and the answer would be a whole lot of nothing. It's still the same old Twitter but now he can harvest user data for his AI pet project. In fact no company to date has made any significant changes to their overall product before or since cutting staffing. They're just standing on what was already there and pocketing the money that would have gone to employees to make it look like they're profitable.

author: @AJax7886
text: So many middle managers are so utterly screwed. They think they'll be getting rid of the machine operators when they are the ones on the chopping block. The guys who play video games all day while someone else does the actual work may finally get the compensation they deserve.

author: @kubi0461
text: One counterpoint… we need to stop normalizing this idea that job environments need to be barren soul-devouring pits of despair.   Also, probably 10% of people at best are the highly motivated workaholic types.   Plus, the jobs that AI is taking are jobs people need in order to gain experience and skills to progress in a career.

author: @solomani-42
text: AI is being used as an excuse to get rid of problematic employees. I work in IT corpo world and AI is not replacing people. It’s supporting/supplementing. But we still fired a bunch of people and they were all problematic one way or another - woke or incompetent mainly.

author: @camgere
text: It would be interesting to for an investigative journalist to interview laid off employees about their jobs. Then analyze how AI has replaced them. I'm extremely skeptical that AI can replace managers. It does generate awesome media. Digital Video and Digital Audio are numbers. AI doesn't give you the right answer, it gives you the most likely answer. It is correct about 80% of the time. I've never had a job where that was acceptable.

author: @Aging_Casually_Late_Gamer
text: The goal of all companies is no workers and all profits

author: @lucidiusdonovan3807
text:
Couldn't agree more with your end-message. AI is a tool that I like to think of as a force multiplier. If your base number is not going up because you do not care about your craft to get really good at it, then that's on you (its just a paycheck mentality). 
Its a lot easier to differentiate 25's versus 5's, as opposed to 3's and 5's versus the do-bare-minimum 1. The coasting-camo is gone.
However - that isn't to say companies aren't taking full advantage and keeping the overwhelming majority of the gains for themselves. I got a 10% bump though I'm now 4x more productive. There's never been a better time to start your own business, the barriers to entry are all but removed. Making amazing progress on my own stuff in my off time. But this opportunity still isn't going to magically make 90% of the population suddenly ambitious enough to exit their comfort zone and try for it

author: @rusttoyoung1912
text: You know what's most pathetic about all of this? These fake activists were given EVERY opportunity to prove their ideals and personal value over the course of five to ten years. They just completely dropped the ball. Yet, even after all of this time and all the firing, the faketivists (the ones who never should've been hired - some of them might've had some talent, who knows), will never admit it was their own fault. They'll just keep playing victim, and contributing nothing.

author: @moonbreath1637
text:
"Ai is going to take some jobs, not all the jobs"
Maybe not this year, but ai is a snowball with nothing to stop it. It's going to keep taking jobs until life is unrecognizable, and then best case scenario humans are integrated and peacefully phased out. Worst case it gets very messy along the way.
People refer back to their sense of normality when thinking about what ai ultimately means for our future, but normality is an illusion.

text: I just dont understand the opposition to AI in gaming.
